varis.name: Varis
varis.desc: Lean and sharp-featured, with dark eyes set deep beneath a heavy brow. His black hair is cropped close to the skull, and his sun-darkened skin is pulled tight over prominent cheekbones. His hands are large-knuckled and callused, with long fingers that move with deliberate economy.

aspects.high_concept: Mysterious Wanderer Who Knows the Void
aspects.trouble: Secrets That Could Help or Harm

dialogue.greeting: A lean figure steps out from behind a cargo crate, watchful but not hostile. 'Another survivor. I was starting to think I was the last one.'
dialogue.idle: 'There's work to be done. The skerry won't grow itself — well, {seed_name} will, but it needs help.'
dialogue.happy: 'This place is starting to feel like it could be something. Keep at it.'
dialogue.quest: 'The frozen wreck to the west... I've seen lights there that shouldn't exist. Be careful if you go.'
dialogue.recruit_success: Varis considers for a moment, then nods. 'A world seed. Haven't seen one of those in a long time. Alright, I'll come. You'll want someone who knows the void.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I've survived out here alone this long. I'll need a better reason than that to move.'

step_early: Varis leans against the crate, arms crossed. 'I'm listening.'
'A world seed.' He doesn't sound impressed. Yet.
He sizes you up. 'I've met plenty of survivors with plans. Most didn't last.'
You describe the skerry. He tilts his head, considering.
'The void takes everything eventually. Why should your place be different?'
step_mid: 'How long has the seed been rooted?' A practical question. Good sign.
He uncrosses his arms. 'Go on.'
'I've seen nodes collapse overnight. What's your contingency?'
He nods slowly. 'You know more than I expected.'
'The others you've recruited. Are they dead weight or useful?'
step_late: 'I know the void routes between here and a dozen nodes. That'd be useful to you.'
He's standing straighter now. Already thinking about logistics.
'Fine. You've made your case.' He almost sounds annoyed about it.
'I've got supplies cached in three locations. I'll retrieve them on the way.'
The skepticism is gone. He's planning.

color.R: 'Defensible position?' He scans the surroundings. 'Tell me about the perimeter.'
color.G: 'World seeds need specific conditions. What's your soil composition?'
color.B: 'Numbers matter. How many hands for watch rotation?'
color.Y: 'Everyone needs a role. What's your chain of command?'
color.O: 'Built to last, or built to last the week?' He fixes you with a stare.

atmosphere: Varis watches you with the flat appraisal of someone who's survived a long time.
Varis is still measuring you, but he hasn't walked away.
Something's shifted. Varis is asking questions, not raising objections.
Varis gives a single, curt nod. He's made up his mind.
warn: Varis's expression cools. You're neglecting something he cares about...
'You were saying?' His tone sharpens. A thread is going thin...
He shifts his weight. You're losing credibility on a front...
eliminate: 'Forget it.' Varis waves a hand. That angle is done.
His jaw tightens. You talked too long without substance and lost him there.
A flat look. That argument died and he's not going to resurrect it for you.

emmy.name: Emmy
emmy.desc: A young woman, barely out of adolescence, with a round face and enormous brown eyes. Her curly light-brown hair is chopped short and uneven — self-cut. Freckles scatter across her nose and cheeks, and her small frame is wiry rather than thin, built for scrambling through tight spaces.

aspects.high_concept: Enthusiastic Scavenger With an Eye for Value

dialogue.greeting: A young woman waves from atop a cargo crate. 'Oh! A person! A real, actual person! I haven't seen anyone in ages!'
dialogue.idle: 'So when do we start scavenging? I know all the good spots around here.'
dialogue.happy: 'I love having people around. The void was so quiet before.'
dialogue.recruit_success: 'You have a place to stay? With actual walls? I'm in. Absolutely in. When do we leave?'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I mean, it sounds nice, but... are you sure it's safe? Maybe come back later?'

step_early: 'Wait, you have a PLACE? Like, with walls?' She bounces on her heels.
You mention other survivors. Emmy's eyes go wide. 'How many?'
'The void is so quiet,' she says. 'I talk to myself just to hear a voice.'
She fidgets with a strap on her pack. 'I've been here so long. Alone.'
You describe the skerry. She leans forward like you're telling a ghost story.
step_mid: 'Real food? Not salvage paste?' She actually gasps.
'Other people. Who are they? What are they like? Tell me everything.'
She starts pacing. A good sign — she's thinking it through.
'I know where all the good scrap is around here. I could be useful!'
'You're serious, aren't you?' She searches your face. 'You're really serious.'
step_late: She's already mentally packing. You can see it in her eyes.
'When do we leave? Can we leave now? I could leave now.'
'I have a stash. Supplies I've been hoarding. I'll bring everything.'
She laughs — sudden, surprised, like she forgot she could. 'I'm in.'
'Tell me about the others again. I want to know their names.'

color.R: You mention walls and shelter. 'Oh THANK the void. I sleep with one eye open here.'
color.G: 'A world seed? A real one?' Her voice cracks with something like hope.
color.B: 'Other people,' she whispers, and you realize she's blinking back tears.
color.Y: 'I'm good at finding things! Scavenging! I could help, I really could.'
color.O: 'Something permanent? Something that lasts?' She grabs your arm. 'Promise me.'

atmosphere: Emmy fidgets, watching you with enormous eyes.
Emmy has stopped fidgeting. She's listening now.
Emmy is bouncing on her heels, barely containing herself.
Emmy looks like she might burst if you don't finish soon.
warn: Emmy's attention is drifting — you're losing a thread...
She glances away. Something you were saying is going stale...
'Hm?' Emmy blinks. You've been neglecting something important...
eliminate: 'Never mind that, I guess,' she says quietly, and that door closes.
Emmy shakes her head. That argument went cold while you weren't looking.
The excitement dims in her eyes. You let that thread go too long.

dax.name: Dax
dax.desc: Stocky and broad-shouldered, with rough, oil-stained hands and close-cropped dark hair. Her face is square-jawed, with a blunt nose and deep-set eyes that narrow when she is thinking. Grease is permanently worked into the creases of her knuckles.

aspects.high_concept: Cautious Mechanic Who's Seen Too Much Go Wrong

dialogue.greeting: A stocky figure looks up from the console they're tinkering with. 'Another survivor? Don't touch anything.'
dialogue.idle: 'If you need something fixed, I'm your person. Just don't expect miracles.'
dialogue.happy: 'I have to admit, this shelter is better than a dead control room. Good work.'
dialogue.recruit_success: Dax looks around the ruined control room, then sighs. 'Fine. Can't be worse than this. Lead the way.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I don't know you. Why would I leave the only shelter I know to follow a stranger? Come back when I have a reason to trust you.'

step_early: Dax doesn't look up from the console. 'Still talking?'
'Yeah, I've heard the pitch before.' A wire sparks. She ignores it.
You mention shelter. She snorts. 'I've GOT shelter.'
'A world seed.' She tightens a bolt. 'Those are supposed to be extinct.'
She holds up a hand. 'If the next thing out of your mouth is a promise, save it.'
step_mid: 'What's the power situation?' A real question. First one.
She sets down the wrench. 'Okay. You have ten more seconds.'
'The seed generates energy?' She looks at you for the first time. Really looks.
'How's the infrastructure? Plumbing? Ventilation? No? Figures.'
'I could fix that. If I were there. Which I'm not. Yet.' A crack in the armor.
step_late: 'This console is held together with wire and spite. Fine. I'll look at your skerry.'
She kicks the console. 'This place is falling apart anyway.'
'You need an engineer. Don't pretend you don't.' She's already packing tools.
'I'm not coming because of your speech. I'm coming because this junk heap is dying.'
She sighs — long, theatrical, defeated. 'Lead the way. Don't make me regret it.'

color.R: 'Structural integrity?' She raps a knuckle on the wall. 'Better than this?'
color.G: 'Organic power source.' She's intrigued despite herself. 'What's the output?'
color.B: 'Great. More people who don't know which end of a wrench to hold.'
color.Y: 'My purpose is keeping things running. That doesn't change with geography.'
color.O: 'Durable? Define durable.' She narrows her eyes. 'In engineering terms.'

atmosphere: Dax tinkers with the console, grunting occasionally in your direction.
Dax has stopped tinkering. She's holding a wrench very still.
Dax is leaning against the console with her arms crossed. Evaluating.
Dax is putting her tools in a bag. She hasn't agreed yet, but her hands have.
warn: Dax picks up the wrench again. You're losing her on something...
'Uh huh.' She's not even pretending to listen to a thread...
She rolls her eyes. Something you dropped is dying fast...
eliminate: 'Nope.' One syllable. Done. That argument is scrap metal.
She snorts derisively. Whatever you were building there just fell apart.
'You lost me.' Back to the console. That thread is welded shut.

chris.name: Chris
chris.desc: A gangly teenager, all elbows and knees, not yet grown into his frame. His ash-blond hair hangs limp over wide, watchful eyes. His face is gaunt — cheekbones too prominent, jaw too sharp — the face of someone who has not eaten well in a long time.

aspects.high_concept: Scared Teenager Trying to Be Brave

dialogue.greeting: A teenager peers out from behind a lean-to, eyes wide. 'Are... are you friendly? Please be friendly.'
dialogue.idle: 'I'm trying to help. Just tell me what to do and I'll do it. Promise.'
dialogue.happy: 'I feel safe here. For the first time in... I don't even know how long.'
dialogue.recruit_success: 'You want me to come with you? Really? Yes! I mean — yes. Definitely. Let's go.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I want to, but... what if it's not safe? What if we get lost on the way?'

step_early: Chris peeks out from behind the lean-to. 'You're... not going to hurt me, right?'
You mention shelter. He swallows hard. 'Real shelter?'
'I haven't eaten real food in... I don't know how long.'
He's shaking slightly. Not cold — just afraid. Of everything.
You keep your voice gentle. He looks like he might bolt.
step_mid: 'There are other people there?' His voice is very small. 'Nice people?'
He uncurls slightly. Still wary, but listening.
'I can work. I'm not useless. I can carry things and — and stuff.'
He takes a half-step closer. Progress.
'Nobody's been nice to me since... before.' He trails off.
step_late: 'You promise it's safe?' His eyes are huge and desperate.
He's already standing. Ready to go. Just needs the last push.
'I'll do anything. Carry things. Clean. Whatever you need. Just let me come.'
'Please.' One word, barely a whisper. He means it with everything he has.
He wipes his eyes with the back of his hand and tries to look brave.

color.R: 'Safe?' He clutches the edge of his lean-to. 'Really safe?'
color.G: You describe the world seed. Chris listens like it's a fairy tale.
color.B: 'Other people...' He shrinks back, then straightens. 'Nice people?'
color.Y: 'I can help,' he says quickly. 'I'm useful. I promise I'm useful.'
color.O: 'Something solid. Something that stays.' He sounds like he's praying.

atmosphere: Chris watches from behind cover, ready to run.
Chris has crept a little closer. Still tense.
Chris is out in the open now, drawn in despite himself.
Chris is standing right in front of you, hope warring with terror.
warn: Chris is shrinking back — you're losing him on something...
His eyes go distant. A thread of the conversation is fraying...
He wraps his arms around himself. Something you dropped is fading...
eliminate: Chris flinches. Whatever you were building there just collapsed.
'Oh.' His voice goes flat. That line of argument is dead.
He takes a step back. You let something important go cold.

tilly.name: Tilly
tilly.desc: A weathered woman in her fifties, with deep brown skin lined by years of sun and wind. Her silver-streaked black hair is pulled back in a practical knot. Her hands are thick-fingered and earth-stained, the nails permanently dark. She is compact and sturdy, built low to the ground.

aspects.high_concept: Practical Farmer Who Makes Things Grow

dialogue.greeting: A weathered woman looks up from her coral garden. 'Hm. You look like you could use a meal. Sit down.'
dialogue.idle: 'The garden needs tending. These coral-seeds don't plant themselves.'
dialogue.happy: 'The soil here responds to {seed_name}. I've never seen anything like it. We could grow real food.'
dialogue.recruit_success: 'You've got a world seed and a place to put down roots? Show me this skerry of yours. If it's real, I'll stay.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'Talk is cheap in the void. Come back when you have something real to show me.'

step_early: Tilly tends her coral garden while she listens. She doesn't look up.
'A world seed, you say.' She brushes soil from her hands. 'I've heard that before.'
'Come back when you have something real.' But she hasn't told you to leave.
She holds up a coral-seed. 'See this? Took me three months to coax it this far.'
You describe the skerry. She pauses mid-work. Brief, but you noticed.
step_mid: 'What's the soil like? Rocky? Sandy?' She actually looks at you now.
'How much sunlight — or whatever passes for it — does the seed get?'
She plucks a weed with practiced fingers. 'I'm listening.'
'Rain? Wind patterns? Don't tell me you haven't checked.'
'If the seed can grow real food, that changes things.' Her tone is careful, guarded.
step_late: 'I'd want to see it first. The soil. Before I commit to anything.'
'My coral-seeds would need transplanting carefully. I'd need help with that.'
She stands up, brushing off her knees. That's the first time she's stopped gardening.
'How soon? I'd need to prepare cuttings. Can't leave anything behind that might root.'
'If half of what you're saying is true...' She trails off, but she's convinced.

color.R: 'Walls keep the wind off seedlings.' Practical, always practical.
color.G: Her head snaps up. 'Describe the growth rate. Exactly.'
color.B: 'More hands for harvest?' She approves. 'Growing food is labor.'
color.Y: 'A garden needs a gardener. That's purpose enough for me.'
color.O: 'Things that last. That's what growing is — building something that outlives you.'

atmosphere: Tilly tends her garden, occasionally glancing your way.
Tilly's hands have slowed. She's thinking more than gardening.
Tilly has set down her tools. You have her full attention.
Tilly is on her feet, already thinking about logistics.
warn: Tilly returns to her weeding. You're losing a thread...
She glances at her garden. Your pitch is going stale on a front...
'Hm.' She's heard prettier words. Something needs reinforcing...
eliminate: She shrugs and returns to weeding. That argument's compost now.
'Not convincing.' She pulls a weed. That line of reasoning is done.
Her expression closes off. You let that one wither on the vine.

callum.name: Callum
callum.desc: Tall and painfully thin, with hollow cheeks and sharp collarbones visible beneath pale skin. His dark hair is unkempt, and he peers through makeshift spectacles of crystal shards wired together. Ink stains his fingers, and the bags under his eyes speak to weeks of insufficient sleep.

dialogue.greeting: A thin man adjusts spectacles made from crystal shards. 'Fascinating — another living being. Tell me, can you read Eliok script?'
dialogue.idle: 'The inscriptions on this ship... they tell a story. A world that grew from a single seed. Sound familiar?'
dialogue.happy: 'Access to {seed_name} changes everything. If I could study the world seed directly...'
dialogue.recruit_success: 'A world seed? An actual living world seed? I need to study it. Take me to it immediately.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I appreciate the offer, but my research here isn't finished. Perhaps once I've translated the vault inscriptions.'

step_early: Callum adjusts his crystal spectacles. 'Interesting. Go on.'
'A world seed. Fascinating.' He says it the way people say 'fascinating' when they mean 'prove it.'
He traces an inscription on the wall while you talk. Only half-listening.
'The Eliok texts describe world seeds as — but you wouldn't know that. Continue.'
You mention the skerry. He makes a small note on a scrap of metal.
step_mid: 'The seed responds to proximity? What's the effective radius?'
He's abandoned the inscriptions entirely. All attention on you now.
'If I could study the root structure directly... the implications for Eliok theory...'
'How mature is the seed? Pre-arboreal? Has it produced secondary growths?'
He cleans his spectacles with trembling hands. He's excited and trying not to show it.
step_late: 'I need to see it. I NEED to see it. Do you understand what this means?'
He's already rolling up his notes. 'The inscriptions can wait. The seed cannot.'
'A living specimen. In the current stage of development. This is unprecedented.'
'Take me there. Immediately. Every hour of data lost is irreplaceable.'
'I'll bring my translation work. The seed and the inscriptions — they're connected. I know it.'

color.R: 'Safety. Yes, obviously. A controlled research environment. Essential.'
color.G: His spectacles nearly fall off. 'GROWTH data? You have growth data?'
color.B: 'A research team? Peers to collaborate with?' He's practically salivating.
color.Y: 'Purpose? My purpose is already clear. Understanding. Knowledge. The seed.'
color.O: 'Stable conditions for long-term observation. Yes. Yes, that's critical.'

atmosphere: Callum traces inscriptions absently, half-listening to you.
Callum has stopped tracing. He's staring at you through those crystal spectacles.
Callum is scribbling notes furiously as you talk.
Callum has rolled up his notes and is standing by the door.
warn: Callum returns to his inscriptions. You're losing his academic interest...
He polishes his spectacles. Not a good sign — he's disengaging from a thread...
'Yes, yes, but—' He's losing patience with something you haven't followed up on...
eliminate: 'Insufficient evidence.' He waves dismissively. That angle is closed.
He returns to his inscriptions. That line of argument bored him to death.
'You had something there, but you didn't develop it.' An academic's disappointment.

lira.name: Lira
lira.desc: A woman in her early thirties with olive skin and steady dark eyes. Her brown hair is sun-bleached at the tips and tied back loosely. Her fingers are stained green at the nails from years of working with plants. She has a strong jaw and a calm, observant face.

dialogue.greeting: Another person. And — what is that tendril on your arm? The vines near you are reaching toward it. I've never seen the biodome react to anything like that.
dialogue.idle: 'Have you tried composting the coral fragments? The mineral content would be extraordinary.'
dialogue.happy: 'The soil on your skerry — it responds to {seed_name} like nothing I've ever studied. I could spend years here.'
dialogue.quest_intro: I've been studying this biodome for weeks. There's something incredible at the heart of it — a crystallized bloom that pulses with raw growth energy. But massive roots block the way north. I would have tried the Growth Controller west of the root wall, but I don't have any tools. Do you?
dialogue.quest_reply_yes: You do? Good. The Growth Control room is west of the root wall. USE your tools ON the CONSOLE — if the logic board is intact, the roots should retract on their own.
dialogue.quest_reply_no: Well, if you have any way to CRAFT some, the Growth Controller is west of the root wall — that's the clean option. Otherwise... coat the roots with resin and burn through. It would work. But these roots are the only thing keeping the biodome alive. I won't let you torch them unless you take me somewhere safe first.
dialogue.quest_options: If you have tools, the Growth Control room is west of the root wall — the console might still work if you can repair it. The roots would retract cleanly. Otherwise... coat the roots with resin and burn through. It would work, but these roots are the biodome's lifeline.
dialogue.quest_careful_done: You preserved the root system! This biodome might survive after all. Here — take these. And if you ever want a botanist on your skerry... I might come visit.
dialogue.quest_forceful_done: You got through. That crystal — it accelerates plant growth. Five uses, maybe. But the root system... you've damaged something irreplaceable.
dialogue.quest_forceful_done_witnessed: I knew what I was suggesting. I just didn't think it would smell like that. ...The crystal has five charges. Let's make them count.
dialogue.quest_complete: That crystal — I've been studying it from this side. It accelerates plant growth. Five uses, maybe, before the energy dissipates.
dialogue.recruit_success: Lira brushes soil from her hands and stands. 'That tendril — whatever it is — it's a living growth source. I've been cataloguing dead biodome fragments for months. This is a chance to study something actually growing.' She's already collecting samples. 'When do we leave?'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I can't leave yet. There's too much data in this biodome — growth patterns, root networks, the crystallization process. Come back when I've finished my survey.'

step_early: Lira glances up from a leaf she's examining, then back down. 'Go on.'
'A living growth source.' She says it like she's testing the words. 'Symbiotic, from the look of it.'
She turns a seedpod over in her fingers while you talk. Only half-listening.
'The biodome's root network is unprecedented. You understand I can't just leave that.'
You describe the skerry. She makes a small note in the margin of her journal.
step_mid: 'The seed produces ambient growth energy?' She sets down the seedpod. 'What radius?'
'Multiple biomes? On one skerry?' She's calculating something in her head.
She pulls off a work glove. 'How mature is the root system?'
'If I could study the interaction between void energy and organic growth in real time...'
'I've been working alone for weeks. Data collection is slow with one pair of hands.'
step_late: 'I could bring cuttings from the biodome. Cross-reference them with your seed's growth patterns.'
She's packing samples into glass vials with practiced speed. Thinking ahead.
'The biodome isn't going anywhere. But that growth organism on your arm — that's a limited window.'
'I need proper lab conditions. Your skerry has soil. It has water. It has a growth source.'
'Fine. Science follows the data, and the data just walked in here and asked me to come.'

color.R: 'Enclosed growing environment?' She nods approvingly. 'Wind kills seedlings.'
color.G: Her eyes go wide. 'Active growth? You've observed active growth? Describe it. Everything.'
color.B: 'Other survivors with different skill sets? That's a research team, not just a settlement.'
color.Y: 'My purpose has always been understanding how things grow. Whatever that organism is, it's the ultimate subject.'
color.O: 'Long-term study requires stability. If the skerry is permanent, the research can be too.'

atmosphere: Lira examines a vine, occasionally glancing your way between notes.
Lira has stopped writing. She's holding her pen very still.
Lira is asking rapid-fire questions. Her journal is forgotten on the bench.
Lira is carefully labeling sample vials. She's already decided.
warn: Lira returns to her notes. You're losing a thread...
She peers at a leaf through a makeshift lens. Something you said is going stale...
'Mm.' She's heard better arguments for that. A thread is fraying...
eliminate: 'Insufficient sample size.' She waves a hand. That angle is dead.
She returns to her journal. Whatever you were building there didn't survive peer review.
A flat look over her spectacles. You let that hypothesis die untested.

angya.name: Angya
angya.desc: Tall and broad-shouldered, with the powerful build of a lifelong fighter. Her thick black hair is worn in two heavy braids. A long scar cuts diagonally across her left cheek. Her dark brown eyes are hard and assessing, set in a wide, weather-beaten face.

aspects.high_concept: Battle-Hardened Warrior of the Old Ships

dialogue.greeting: A scarred woman rises from a crouch, hand on a makeshift blade. 'State your business. Quickly.'
dialogue.idle: 'Don't bore me with talk. If there's fighting to be done, I'm ready.'
dialogue.happy: 'This skerry... it's worth defending. I'll make sure nothing threatens it.'
dialogue.recruit_success: Angya sheathes her blade. 'You fight well. That's all I needed to see. Lead on.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'You want me to follow you? Prove you're worth following. Fight me.'

step_early: Angya's hand rests on her blade. She hasn't decided if you're worth listening to.
'Words.' She says it like a curse. 'Everyone has words.'
You press on. She watches with the patience of someone used to waiting for an opening.
'I don't need a speech. I need a reason.' Her eyes are flint.
She shifts her weight. Fighter's habit — always ready to move.
step_mid: 'How many hostiles have you dealt with?' A real question. She's engaging.
Her grip on the blade relaxes fractionally.
'The skerry. Can it be defended?' She's thinking tactically.
'I've been fighting alone. That gets old.' The closest she'll come to admitting loneliness.
She rolls her shoulders. 'Keep talking.'
step_late: 'You need someone who can hold a line. I can hold a line.'
She sheathes the blade. The decision is made, even if she hasn't said it.
'I've been wasted out here guarding nothing. Give me something worth defending.'
A dangerous grin. 'Your enemies are my enemies. That's all I needed to hear.'
'Fine. But I fight MY way. We clear on that?'

color.R: 'Shelter's no good if you can't defend it.' She tests you.
color.G: She eyes you sidelong at the mention of growth. Seeds aren't her language, but survival is.
color.B: 'More fighters?' Her interest sharpens. 'Or more mouths?'
color.Y: 'Purpose.' She snorts. 'My purpose is keeping people alive. Works everywhere.'
color.O: 'Built solid?' She kicks a wall experimentally. 'Good enough.'

atmosphere: Angya stands ready, hand on blade, judging every word.
Angya is still armed, but she's stopped looking for exits.
Angya's posture has shifted from combat stance to something almost relaxed.
Angya's blade is sheathed. She's already decided.
warn: Angya's hand drifts back to her blade. You're boring her...
Her eyes go hard. Talk without substance is losing you ground...
'Get to the point.' She's losing patience with a thread...
eliminate: 'Done talking about that.' She cuts the air with her hand. Final.
Her lip curls. You let that argument die and she's not sorry to see it go.
A disgusted headshake. Words wasted. That door is shut.
